coredata.dc:title: Multiple functionalized carbon quantum dots for targeting glioma and tissue imaging
coredata.dc:creator: Gao, Lipeng
Zhao, Xiao
Wang, Jing
Wang, Yiting
Yu, Lei
Peng, Hui
Zhu, Jianzhong
coredata.dc:description: Carbon quantum dots (CQDs) was successfully functionalized with Mal-PEG-NHS linked RGERPPR. They exhibit double functions of both tissue imaging and targeting to brain gliomas. The mean size of the functionalized CQDs about 9.0 ± 2.0 nm. The maximum absorption wavelength of the functionalized CQDs appear at 230 nm. The peak of the fluorescence spectra for the functionalized CQDs is at 460 nm, red shifted by 20 nm comparing with the unmodified CQDs. This may be due to the increased particle size. The functionalized CQDs were successfully applied to imaging and targeting gliomas.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Carbon quantum dots
Glioma targeting
Tissue imaging
Mal-PEG-NHS
RGERPPR
Blood brain barrier

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: absattachment articleinfo articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issn issnnorm itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pglast pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sectiontitle sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content subj subheadings suppl tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav figure body acknowledge affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast grantnumber grantsponsor grantsponsorid highlightsabst primabst ref specialabst

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: NMR spectra (A) and FTIR spectra (B) of the functionalized CNPs.
The size distribution of the functionalized CQDs targeting to gliomas. The particles with the diameter between 7 and 11 nm accounted for about 90% of the total number.
Ultraviolet Absorption Spectra of the functionalized CQDs targeting gliomas. The location of the peak is at 230 nm. The absorbance decrease quickly with the increase of the excitation wavelength.

Fluorescence emission spectra of the functionalized CQDs targeting gliomas at different wavelength excitation: 1) 300, 2) 320, 3) 340, 4) 360 and 5) 380 nm. PL intensity increase with decrease of excitation wavelength and the of the fluorescence emission peaks move slightly toward violet direction. Inset: normalized emission spectra.
Photos of nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR). A is the experimental group and B the control group. It was seen that there is a gliomas with 2.2 width and 2.4 height in A, no gliomas in B.
Photo of brain gliomas (diameter about 2.29 mm) taking out from a nude mice inoculated U87 cell.

Fluorescence imaging of frozen section of the brain tumor for the experiment group (A) and (B) for the normal tissue the nude mice was received a tail vein injection of the certain amount of the functionalized CQDs solution, as well as (C) for the control group (the nude mice was only received a tail vein injection of same amount of saline).
Structure of the functionalized CQD proposed. Fragment 1, Mal-PEG-NHS, Fragment 2, RGERPPR, Fragment 3, CQD.

head.ce:title: Multiple functionalized carbon quantum dots for targeting glioma and tissue imaging

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering, East China Normal University, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Carbon quantum dots (CQDs) was successfully functionalized with Mal-PEG-NHS linked RGERPPR. They exhibit double functions of both tissue imaging and targeting to brain gliomas. The mean size of the functionalized CQDs about 9.0 ± 2.0 nm. The maximum absorption wavelength of the functionalized CQDs appear at 230 nm. The peak of the fluorescence spectra for the functionalized CQDs is at 460 nm, red shifted by 20 nm comparing with the unmodified CQDs. This may be due to the increased particle size. The functionalized CQDs were successfully applied to imaging and targeting gliomas.

Structure of the functionalized CQD proposed. Fragment 1, Mal-PEG-NHS, Fragment 2, RGERPPR, Fragment 3, CQD. A carbon quantum dots (CQDs) was successfully functionalized with Mal-PEG-NHS linked RGERPPR. They exhibit double functions of both tissue imaging and targeting to brain gliomas. The mean size of the functionalized CQDs about 9.0 ± 2.0 nm. The maximum absorption wavelength of the functionalized CQDs appear at 230 nm. The peak of the fluorescence spectra for the functionalized CQDs is at 460 nm, red shifted by 20 nm comparing with the unmodified CQDs. This may be due to the increased particle size. The functionalized CQDs were successfully applied to imaging and targeting gliomas.
Image 1

ce:section.ce:para: Brain glioma (BG) is the most common primary central nervous system tumors, accounting for about 46% of intracranial primary tumors [1,2], which greatly threatens human life and health. The first choice in clinical treatment at present is the direct surgical resection after controlling the conditions by radiotherapy and chemotherapy. However, radiotherapy cannot kill all the tumor cells. According to the literature, one-hundred-thousandth of the tumor cells left will rapidly proliferate into tumor tissue, and the proliferation cycle is usually less than a week [2–5]. Therefore, postoperative radiotherapy or chemotherapy on the treatment of glioma is crucial. Compared with other solid tumors, the glioma has its own characteristics. Enhanced permeability and retention [6], followed by the presence of the blood brain barrier [7,8], make it very difficult for most drugs to enter tumor tissue and kill tumor cells. So intracranial targeting drug delivery systems are one of the most effective ways for the treatment of glioma [9–13]. Among these systems, nano-drug delivery systems based on receptor-mediated transfer into the brain are very attractive. Combining transferrin receptor-related proteins, such as Transferrin (Tf) and Lactoferrin (Lf), and specific receptors can temporarily open the blood-brain barrier bound.

Carbon quantum dots (CQDs) are widely used for biosensing and bioimaging due to their excellent biocompatibility, low cost and easy preparation. Different methods have been developed to prepare CQDs with excellent characteristics by using various precursors, including silk, glucose and amino acids [14–17]. To image the brain tumors, many researchers focused on constructing nanoparticle systems that were functionalized with brain tumor targeting ligands [18–20]. For example, Benzhong Tang's group reported that aggregation-induced emission fluorogens (AIEgens) exhibit excellent performances for tissue imaging and tumor targeting [21,22]. Zang et al. developed AgInZnS-graphene oxide (AIZS-GO) QDs can be used as an active biomarker for biomedical imaging [23]. However, tethering the targeting molecules onto the CQDs still remains a great challenge for constructing a perfect “smart” theranostic agent, which is a multiple functional platform integrating imaging, targeting, and therapeutic functions.

It is well known that the tumor-penetrating peptide, RGERPPR, can penetrate the tumor tissue vascular wall and then targets to glioma [24–27]. Therefore, in this paper, we functionalized CQDs with maleimide-polyethylene glycol-aminosuccinimide succinate (Mal-PEG-NHS) and the tumor-penetrating peptide to construct a multiple functional platform for tissue imaging and targeting to glioma.

Hank's balanced salt solution (HBSS, calcium ion-free and magnesium ion-free), phosphate buffer solution (PBS), 0.25% trypsin (EDTA-free), dimethylsulfoxide (DMSO) and DMEM cell culture medium (high glucose, 1% Non-essential amino acids, 1% l-glutamine, 110 mg/L sodium pyruvate) were purchased from Gibco, USA. Fetal bovine serum (FBS) was purchased from Biological Industries, Israel, origin of Australia. Penicillin-Streptomycin Solution, was available from HyClone. Hydrochloric acid propranolol, (content ≥ 95%) purchased from Shanghai Weita Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. Female Kunming mice (age 8 weeks) was available from of Institute of Brain Function, East China Normal University. Caco-2 cell line (40 and 60 generations) was purchased from Shanghai Life cell library, Chinese Academy of Sciences.

CQDs were fabricated according to previous paper [28]. Briefly, 4.32 g of citric acid monohydrate and 9 ml of dethylene glycol bis (3-aminopropyl) ether in a three neck flask were reacted at 400 °C for 2 h under vigorous magnetic stirring and nitrogen protection. During the reaction, the color of the solution turned from colorless to yellow, reddish brown and final black. After cooling, a little of water was added into the flask, which was centrifuged at 4 °C, 8000 revolutions per minute for 5 min. Repeated the above steps 3 times, supernatant fluid until no precipitation. The supernatant fluid was transferred in a dialysis bag with 500 molecular intercept to dialysis for 7 days. The CQDs solution was diluted to 50 ml by water to form a stock solution for stepwise dilution.

ce:list-item.ce:para: Recovery of U87 cells to the culture dish and cultured to the third passage, the cell suspension solution was prepared with PBS and the density of the U87 cells was adjusted to 4 × 107/ml with the cell count instrument;

The nude mice was fasted for 12 h before preoperative, and injected intraperitoneally by 2% pentobarbital solution according to 3 mg/kg, then the nude mice was fixed on the brain stereotaxic instrument;

After alcohol disinfection of the skin between the top of the head and the outer ear, make 0.5–1 cm of vertical incision at the middle line and behind the eye crack of the nude mice, exposure to the anterior fontanel, take 1 mm before the midpoint of the anterior fontanel, 3 mm of the right to the sagittal suture and subdural 5 mm as a target. With a diameter of 1 mm dental drill, drill through the skull;

10 μL of cell suspension as-prepared was injected with 20 μL micro-injector. The needle was slowly and vertically entered into the brain for 6 mm and back 1 mm. Injection time was 10 min and the needle was hold for 5 min after finishing the injection. The bone hole was closed with bone wax, the operating field was rinsed with saline and the scalp was sutured;
Raise the temperature to 30 °C until the nude mice wake up, and then regular feeding;

Six days after inoculation, six nude mice were randomly selected for nuclear magnetic resonance examination; daily observation of nude mice activity and eating, regular measurement of body weight, record the survival time;
After the 16 days of inoculation, the functionalized CQDs was injected into the nude mice through tail vein, continuous injection for 3 days, with saline as a control;

The nude mice were killed by excessive anesthesia and the glioma was took out after dissection of the brain, the specimens were embedded with tissue embedding agent, frozen sections, placed under it fluorescence microscope.

ce:section.ce:para: The morphology of the functionalized CQDs was characterized by a JEOL JSM-6700F Electron Microscope (Japan), HRTEM (Hitachi H-9000) and a D/MAX 2550V with Cu Kα radiation, respectively. Cell counter and Millicell-ERS volt-ohmmeter (Merck Millipore), Ultraviolet spectrophotometer, Fluorescence spectrophotometer (OLYMPUS IMAGING) and high performance liquid phase chromatography (HPLC) (shimadzu Co., LTD, Japan), laser particle analyzer, mastersizer 2000 (Malvern Instrument LTD), Freezing microtome (Leica), Multiskan MK3 Automatic enzyme standard instrument, Embedded culture wells, Transwell Millipore Ltd. Co., Cell resistance meter, Millipore and the cell constant temperature incubator (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were used for relevant test.

The structure of the functionalized CQDs was shown in Scheme 1. It consists of three fragments, Mal-PEG-NHS, RGERPPR and CQD. The maleimide group selectively forms a stable thioether bond with the free sulfur-based bond of the active drug under mild conditions, neutral or weakly acidic. Succinimidyl succinate is bonded to the free amino group on the surface of a carbon quantum dot to form an aminosuccinate bond.
The structure of the functionalized CQDs was confirmed by NMR spectra (B) and FTIR spectra (C) (See Fig. 1 A and B).

The functionalized CQDs had a uniform spherical shape and a narrow size distribution of 9.0 ± 2.0 nm in diameter, slightly larger 12.5% than one of the CQDs previously proposed [28]. The size distribution of the functionalized CQDs targeting to gliomas was shown in Fig. 2 . The particles with the diameter between 7 and 11 nm accounted for about 90% of the total number.

Ultraviolet Absorption Spectra of the functionalized CQDs targeting gliomas was shown in Fig. 3 . The location of the peak is at 230 nm with a concentration of 340 mM like the CQDs. The absorbance decrease quickly with the increase of the excitation wavelength.

The curves 1–5 in Fig. 4 shows the PL emission spectra of the functionalized CQDs targeting gliomas dispersion with various excitation wavelengths from 300 to 380 nm. Note that a strong PL emission spectrum centered at 440 nm was observed when it was excited at 300 nm, indicating the production of fluorescent functionalized CQDs targeting gliomas. The image of the dispersion under UV light (300 nm) exhibits an obvious blue color, further revealing that the resultant the functionalized CQDs targeting gliomas exhibit strong blue fluorescence.

It is seen that the peaks of emission spectrum of the functionalized CQDs targeting gliomas thus obtained changes from 437.9 nm to 443.6 nm, with a dependence on the excitation wavelengths from 300 to 400 nm. The emission peak position shifts to longer wavelengths and the intensity gradually decreases with increased excitation wavelengths (Fig. 4 Inset). Such observation is similar to that of CQDs, which may be attributed to the optical selection of differently sized nanoparticles (quantum effect) or different emissive traps on the surface or another mechanism altogether. The full width at half-maximum (FWHM) at 300 nm is about 72.9 nm, indicating the narrow size distribution of the functionalized CQDs size. Furthermore, the quantum yield of functionalized CQDs is 11% through calculation.

Fluorescence imaging of frozen sections of the brain tumor (A) and normal tissue (B) (the nude mice were received a tail vein injection of the certain amount of the functionalized CQDs) as well as control group C (only received a tail vein injection of same amount of saline) were shown in Fig. 7 to further elucidate the distribution of the functionalized CQDs in brain. The functionalized CQDs displayed higher localization in glioma, while much lower distribution in the brain of the normal tissue and the control group were observed. This result suggested the functionalized CQDs could target to brain glioma (Fig. 7A) rather than normal brain tissues (Fig. 7B), owing to the enhanced permeability and retention (EPR) effect in glioma and the integrity of blood-brain barrier (BBB) in normal brain tissues [29,30]. Therefore, the functionalized CQDs could be used for glioma-targeted diagnosis with low toxicity to normal brain. Anchoring with specific ligands, especially glioma homing peptides, may further improve the glioma targeting efficiency of the functionalized CQDs [19].

The tumor-penetrating peptide RGERPPR should be the ideal targeting molecules for the tumor-targeted drug delivery system. This is because that the receptor of RGERPPR, Neuropilin-1, is distributed both on tumor cells and tumor blood vessels, and more importantly that the RGERPPR can penetrate through the tumor vessel wall and tumor stroma [31–33]. The penetration through the tumor vessel wall can break the limitation of the EPR effect, so that the nano-drug delivery system can enter tumor tissue by virtue of not only the EPR effect, but also the penetrating ability of the RGERPPR.

CQDs were synthesized and functionalized with Mal-PEG-NHS linked RGERPPR. They exhibits double functions of both tissue imaging and targeting to brain gliomas. The mean size of the complex NPs is about 9.0 ± 2.0 nm, bigger than 12.5% of the CQDs previously proposed. The maximum absorption wavelength of the functionalized CQDs appear at 230 nm which is almost same with one of the CQDs previously proposed. The peak of the fluorescence spectra for the functionalized CQDs is at 460 nm, red shifted by 20 nm comparing with the CQDs. This may be due to the larger size of the functionalized CQDs. The U87 cell suspension was injected into the nude mice. After tumor formation, the tumor was characterized by NMR. The functionalized CQDs were injected by tail vein injection. The tumor-penetrating peptide (RGERPPR) can penetrate the tumor tissue vascular wall and then targets to glioma. Frozen sections of tumor tissue were measured by a fluorescence microscope to observe the fluorescence. A clear photo of gliomas was obtained, indicating that the targeting gliomas of the functionalized CQDs was successful. The functionalized CQDs will be linked with an anti-cancer medicine to expanse function of the functionalized CQDs, which is a future task.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The project is supported by National Natural Science Foundation of China (60976004, 61076070) and Shanghai Municipal Commission for Science and Technology (11JC1403800).
